releases.title: collectZ v3.19.4
releases.summary: This patch makes Library saved views durable. Saved views now sync through authenticated, scope-aware backend endpoints when available, while keeping browser-local saved views as a fallback for older or unavailable backends.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a `saved_library_views` persistence table.
Added scoped Library saved-view list, create, update, and delete endpoints.
Updated the Library saved-view controls to load backend views first and fall back to local storage.
Preserved the 3.19.3 saved-view snapshot shape for search, filters, sort, display mode, and library-specific context.
Added unit/source coverage for the durable saved-view contract.

releases.title: collectZ v3.19.3
releases.summary: This patch adds browser-local saved views to the Library. Users can save the current search, filters, sort, card/list mode, and library-specific context, then reopen that view later from the same browser.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added local saved Library views scoped by media type.
Added compact Library header controls to save/update, apply, and delete saved views.
Saved search text, quick filters, review filters, sort direction, card/list mode, collection mode, and comic view context where relevant.
Added unit/source coverage for the saved-view contract.

releases.title: collectZ v3.19.2
releases.summary: This patch makes the existing Library search box identifier-aware. Users can search by stored UPC/EAN/ISBN-style identifiers, TMDB ids, provider ids, Plex keys, and identity-alias metadata without leaving the current library surface.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Extended `GET /api/media` search to match stored UPC, ISBN-style type details, TMDB ids, provider ids, Calibre/Kavita ids, Plex metadata keys, and identity aliases.
Guarded numeric-normalized matching so non-numeric searches do not become broad empty-string identifier matches.
Updated the Library search placeholder to advertise identifier-aware search.
Added unit/source coverage for the identifier search contract.

releases.title: collectZ v3.19.1
releases.summary: This patch fixes Plex movie imports after media cleanup. Plex movie rows no longer carry TV-only fields from the Plex payload, so deleted movie titles can be recreated without tripping the media TV-field consistency constraint.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Stopped mapping Plex movie `studio` values into the collectZ `network` field.
Stopped carrying Plex `parentIndex` into `season_number` for non-TV rows.
Added unit coverage proving normalized Plex movie rows keep TV-only fields empty.

releases.title: collectZ v3.19.0
releases.summary: This release makes library bulk delete behave like one intentional operation instead of hundreds of individual delete requests. The change protects normal cleanup workflows from tripping API rate limits while keeping scoped authorization and audit readback intact.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added `POST /api/media/bulk-delete` for confirmed bulk media deletes.
Preserved active workspace/library scoping and the same ownership rules as single-record delete.
Added per-operation counts for requested, deleted, skipped, and failed records.
Updated the Library bulk delete UI to call the bulk endpoint once instead of firing one request per selected row.
Documented the new endpoint in OpenAPI.
Added a targeted smoke test proving more than 200 selected media rows can be deleted through one request.
Updated the frontend build toolchain patch versions to clear the current `esbuild` audit finding.
Fixed an event autograph artifact regression found during browser verification where creating an autograph artifact could fail before returning its signature readback.

releases.title: collectZ v3.18.6
releases.summary: This patch expands the generated public mirror beyond install/support files by adding the audited frontend source surface and public OpenAPI contract to the export manifest. Backend implementation source remains private.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added `backend/openapi/` to the public export allowlist.

Added curated frontend source/build files to the public export allowlist: `frontend/index.html`, `frontend/package.json`, `frontend/package-lock.json`, `frontend/src/`, `frontend/tailwind.config.js`, and `frontend/vite.config.js`.
Updated public export validation policy to allow those audited paths while continuing to deny backend implementation, private CI, maintainer docs, artifacts, generated builds, and local overrides.
Updated maintainer public-repository documentation to reflect the new source-publication boundary.

releases.title: collectZ v3.18.5
releases.summary: This patch adds public-safe runtime contract names for auth responses and moves frontend/OpenAPI usage to those names. The backend keeps the previous fields for compatibility, while the candidate public source audit now reaches zero findings.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added `runtime_mode` and `runtime_contract` aliases to auth config and user-session responses.
Updated the frontend to prefer `runtime_mode` while retaining a compatibility fallback for older responses.
Updated OpenAPI to document the public-safe runtime contract names.
Reduced the public-source boundary audit from `12` findings across `3` files to `0` findings.

releases.title: collectZ v3.18.4
releases.summary: This patch replaces frontend helper and state names that used private deployment language with neutral local-runtime wording. It keeps the existing backend contract value intact, but makes the frontend source less tied to private product labels.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Renamed frontend runtime helper names away from private deployment terminology.
Renamed navigation and dashboard state variables to use `localRuntime` wording.
Preserved the existing backend `product_edition` contract behavior for compatibility.
Reduced the public-source boundary audit from `27` findings across `5` files to `12` findings across `3` files.

releases.title: collectZ v3.18.3
releases.summary: This patch removes the remaining `control plane` wording from the candidate public source surfaces. It is one step in making the public mirror useful enough to include frontend source and API contract files later.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Reworded the admin workspace management description in the frontend.
Reworded four OpenAPI admin workspace summaries away from internal operations language.
Reduced the public-source boundary audit from `32` findings across `6` files to `27` findings across `5` files.
Confirmed the `control plane` blocker category is cleared from the audit.

releases.title: collectZ v3.18.2
releases.summary: This release adds the first explicit public-source boundary audit. The public mirror remains an install/support mirror for now, but maintainers can now measure exactly which frontend and OpenAPI source surfaces still block a useful source mirror.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added `npm run audit:public-source-boundary` to scan candidate source-publication surfaces.
Added a JSON audit report under ignored local artifacts so blockers can be reviewed without committing evidence files.
Tracked blocker categories for private runtime language, runtime contract names, operations wording, auth/test bypass labels, and private docs references.
Updated public mirror docs and sanitizer strategy to make clear that application source remains private until this boundary is intentionally cleaned.
Tightened the public export contract so source folders stay denied until the audit findings are resolved.
